proposal.title: Transitive blast radius includes depth-1 entries, duplicating direct dependents
proposal.whyThisBoundary: reviewDiff partitions blast radius entries into direct (depth===1) and transitive (depth>1) arrays. The blue team tests use toContain checks on each array but never assert mutual exclusivity or that totalEntities equals direct.length + transitive.length. Changing the transitive filter from depth>1 to depth>=1 causes depth-1 entries to appear in BOTH arrays — a semantic duplication the tests cannot detect.
proposal.survivalRationale: With mutation, sum (depth 1) appears in both direct and transitive. Test line 393 checks directNames.toContain('sum') — still passes. Test line 397 checks transitiveNames.toContain('formatTotal') — still passes (formatTotal at depth 2 still included). The maxDepth test at line 404 checks shallowAll.not.toContain('formatTotal') — still passes because formatTotal isn't in the blast at maxDepth=1. No test checks that direct and transitive are disjoint or that their combined length equals totalEntities.
proposal.validatorNotes: Single operator change: > becomes >=. Only affects the transitive filter in reviewDiff. The direct filter (depth===1) is unchanged. totalEntities is computed from blastEntries.length (unchanged), so it would no longer equal direct.length + transitive.length — an invariant violation no test catches.
